message:
feat(http): add HTTP/1.1 keep-alive connection reuse to HttpClient

HttpClient previously opened a fresh TCP connection for every request.
This commit introduces idle-connection pooling so that HTTP/1.1
connections are reused when the server signals keep-alive.

Key changes:

1. Http1Client::request_with_io -- new public method that returns
   (Response, T) instead of just Response, giving the caller back the
   transport after the response body is fully drained. The original
   request method delegates to it for backward compat.

2. ClientIncomingBody::into_inner -- exposes the underlying I/O after
   body consumption, enabling transport hand-back.

3. HttpClient gains an idle_connections: Mutex<HashMap<PoolKey,
   Vec<(u64, ClientIo)>>> field. The execute_single path now:
   a) tries acquire_connection which checks the pool for an idle
      connection before dialing a new one;
   b) after a successful response, calls release_connection if
      connection_can_be_reused (HTTP/1.1 without Connection: close,
      or HTTP/1.0 with explicit Connection: keep-alive);
   c) on error, calls drop_connection to discard the transport.

4. Helper functions connection_can_be_reused, header_has_token, and
   pool_now support the reuse decision.

5. Unit tests cover request_with_io transport reuse, header_has_token
   CSV matching, and connection_can_be_reused for both HTTP/1.0 and
   HTTP/1.1 semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
